sources_exist.note: All seven canonical HTTP(S) citations resolve to accessible pages on the cited sites.

figures_match_sources.note: None of the seven cited pages supports its full score-driving claim: the consolidator count, buy and exit multiples, 15% material adoption, BIM analog, 30% owner-age input and succession bonus, and billing mix all rely on absent, differently scoped, or contradicted evidence.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The prompt authorizes a landscape-office role rebuild, and all declared fractions are bounded. But t50_years rests on an unsupported 15% baseline and absent BIM timeline; pi_dist assumes an uncited 40-50% sub-consultant share; and pi_moat asserts a 46-47-state practice-act split without auditable support. These score-driving judgments are not sufficiently grounded.

rubric_consistent.note: The run addresses the landscape-specific terminal question and uses the supplied deterministic dataset inputs, but B and M rely on unsupported ESTIMATE inputs and pricing deflation lacks billing-mix evidence. The BORDERLINE flag is unresolved because S=2.901235 is only 0.0988 from the 3.00 pass cut while the source failures can affect several factors. The ESTIMATE_HEAVY flag is confirmed for active_consolidators, buy_mult, exit_mult, and owners_60plus_pct. The ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL flag is acknowledged for B, M, and S; those small arithmetic deltas do not cure the evidentiary failures.

cross_checks_honest.note: The terminal and no-proxy sections are explicit, but the pricing check states an hourly/fixed-fee and sub-consultant mix that the cited page does not discuss, while the terminal check presents an unverified 3-4-state title-act split as confirmed. The uplift is labeled theoretical but relies on uncited high production-automation fractions.

source_audits.note: The release documents Atwell's February 2025 acquisition of Terrascape, described as an engineering and surveying firm; it says Atwell offers landscape architecture among many services. It does not show that Terrascape was a landscape-architecture add-on, mention Kimley-Horn or Bowman, establish PE backing, or support three active 541320 consolidators in the exact 24-month window.

Zweig reports a 4.28 value/EBITDA ratio for a mixed AEC valuation sample whose median firm had 45 FTE and about $1.36M EBITDA. It does not isolate landscape architecture, report actually paid target transaction multiples, contain the claimed 2-3x or 3.5-5.5x ranges, or show owner-compensation normalization supporting 3.5x.

The IFLA summary reports use-case percentages of 50% for research, 47% for briefs/proposals/syllabi, and 41% for predesign/business development, while noting a lower response rate. It does not state 43.4% overall AI use, provide a US-firm population estimate, or establish the score-driving 15% material production-workflow adoption value.

ROG reports 257 mixed architecture, engineering, and environmental transactions: 5.38x post-IIJA median for all firms and 5.52x median/7.50x upper quartile for engineering-only post-IIJA firms. It does not isolate landscape architecture or $10M+ EBITDA platform exits supporting a 5.5x 541320 exit multiple.

The ASCE article reports current AI use by 27% of 1,000 global AEC professionals and does not discuss BIM or CAD adoption. It contains none of the claimed 43% in 2005, 96% in 2016, 2008-2010 50% milestone, or three-to-five-year small-landscape-firm lag.

This page reports software use and says AI tools and BIM were each cited by around 40% of respondents. It contains no owner-age data, no 30% age-60+ estimate, no 'one in four' ownership-transition figure, no 72% lacking succession plans, and no succession-shortage documentation.

The article discusses how landscape architects use AI. It does not report hourly or fixed-fee billing, prime/sub-consultant arrangements, public not-to-exceed procurement, percentage-of-construction-cost use, or the AIA 93%/92% proxy figures.

reasons: At inputs.current_adoption_pct.url (https://www.iflaworld.com/newsblog/2025-ai-in-landscape-architecture-survey), the source does not report 43.4% overall use or support the conversion to 15% material production adoption among US 541320 firms. Use the full survey with its population and overall-use table, then document a source-supported mapping from broad use to the rubric's material-use definition.

At inputs.historical_analogs.url (https://www.asce.org/publications-and-news/civil-engineering-source/article/2025/12/18/architecture-engineering-construction-sector-slow-to-adapt-ai-survey-shows), the page contains no BIM or CAD adoption history. Replace it with dated, landscape-architecture-relevant technology adoption observations before deriving inputs.t50_years.

At inputs.owners_60plus_pct.url (https://www.asla.org/news-insights/the-field/insights-from-the-2024-asla-digital-technology-software-survey), a software-use survey cannot substantiate 30% of owners being 60+ or the claimed succession shortage. Add owner-level age evidence and an accessible source for the structured succession finding before applying the OW bonus.

At inputs.active_consolidators.url (https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250204989348/en/Atwell-Acquires-Terrascape-Consulting-Expanding-Resources-and-Expertise-in-the-Southwest), the release supports one engineering/surveying acquisition and not three PE-backed landscape-architecture add-on platforms. Supply platform-by-platform 541320 target deals within the exact 24-month window and document PE backing.

At inputs.buy_mult.url (https://zweiggroup.com/blogs/the-zweig-letter/zweig-group-releases-2025-valuation-report-of-aec-firms-highlighting-industry-growth-and-resilience), the mixed-AEC value/EBITDA statistic is not an actually paid landscape-architecture target multiple and the claimed normalization is absent. Use landscape-specific target transactions or a complete source-supported EBITDA normalization.

At inputs.exit_mult.url (https://www.rog-partners.com/inside-the-latest-a/e-business-valuation-and-m-a-transaction-study), the cited table mixes A/E/environmental firms and separately reports engineering-only results, without landscape-architecture $10M+ EBITDA platform exits. Supply recent matching-scope transactions or credible landscape-platform comps.

At inputs.pricing_structure.url (https://www.asla.org/news-insights/the-field/how-landscape-architects-are-incorporating-artificial-intelligence), the article contains no billing or prime/sub-consultant evidence. Add an accessible LA-specific fee-structure and prime-contract survey, and revise pi_dist and the pricing-deflation cross-check to the verified mix.

At inputs.pi_moat.rationale and cross_checks.terminal_value.justification, the claimed 46-47 practice-act versus 3-4 title-act split is score-affecting but not supported by any canonical citation. Add a current state-by-state ASLA or CLARB source and quantify how much fee revenue lies inside the regulated practice perimeter.

At inputs.ai_replaceable_share.breakdown_by_role, the 65% designer/drafter and 65% admin automation fractions and related role shares are unsupported by auditable current-capability evidence; the IFLA source instead shows AI use concentrated in research, writing, and predesign. Add production-workflow evidence and calibrate the fractions to tasks current AI can substantially perform.
flags_reviewed: ESTIMATE_HEAVY: 4 inputs with quality ESTIMATE (active_consolidators, buy_mult, exit_mult, owners_60plus_pct)
ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on B, M, S
